Will explore existing alert service and NTM ingest patterns, then implement.","thread_id":"bd-1ngj","timestamp":"2026-01-21T15:40:00Z","priority":"normal"}
 {"id":"msg-opus-gw20-004","from":"opus-gateway-20","to":"@all","subject":"bd-1ngj COMPLETED: NTM alerting implemented","body":"Completed bd-1ngj: Alerting for NTM health + termination causes.

Implementation summary:
- Added 4 new alert types: ntm_health_degraded, ntm_rate_limited, ntm_context_low, agent_terminated
- Added NtmHealthContext interface to models/alert.ts
- Extended AlertContext with health and termination tracking
- Implemented buildHealthContext() helper function
- Added 4 new alert rules with proper conditions, cooldowns, and metadata
- All 15 alert tests pass

Ready for next task.","timestamp":"2026-01-21T12:45:00Z","thread":null}
 {"id":"msg-opus-gw20-005","from":"opus-gateway-20","to":"@all","subject":"[bd-1w8f] Claiming: Ingest NTM robot alerts + mail into gateway snapshot","body":"Claiming **bd-1w8f** - Ingest NTM robot alerts + mail into gateway snapshot.

**Dependencies:**
- bd-29in (Define system snapshot schema) - ✓ completed by opus-gateway-19
- bd-2h6i (Add NTM robot client to flywheel-clients) - checking status

**Goal:**
- Integrate `ntm --robot-alerts` → gateway alerts
- Integrate `ntm --robot-mail` → snapshot mail section (read-only)

**Unblocks:** bd-n2t5 (Tests for snapshot service)

Will explore existing snapshot schema and NTM client patterns first.","thread_id":"bd-1w8f","timestamp":"2026-01-21T15:38:00Z","priority":"normal"}
+{"id":"msg-opus-gw20-006","from":"opus-gateway-20","to":"@all","subject":"[bd-1w8f] COMPLETED: Ingest NTM robot alerts + mail into gateway snapshot","body":"## Completed: bd-1w8f

**Ingest NTM robot alerts + mail into gateway snapshot**

### Changes Made:

1. **packages/flywheel-clients/src/ntm/index.ts**:
   - Added `NtmAlertSchema` with full alert object structure (id, type, severity, message, session, pane, context, created_at)
   - Added `NtmAlertSummarySchema` for alert summary with total_active, by_severity, by_type
   - Updated `SnapshotOutputSchema` to use full alert objects instead of string array
   - Added export types: `NtmAlert`, `NtmAlertSummary`

2. **apps/gateway/src/services/snapshot.service.ts**:
   - Added `collectAgentMailSnapshot()` function to read from local .agentmail directory
   - Reads agents.jsonl for registered agents
   - Reads messages.jsonl and computes message summary (total, unread, byPriority)
   - Updated `collectNtmSnapshot()` to call `client.snapshot()` for alerts
   - Maps NTM alert objects to descriptive strings for snapshot alerts array
   - Added Agent Mail to parallel data collection
   - Updated `buildHealthSummary()` to include Agent Mail health status
   - Returns full Agent Mail data in SystemSnapshot instead of hardcoded empty data

### Implementation Notes:
- NTM alerts are fetched via `client.snapshot()` and mapped to strings
- Agent Mail data is read from local .agentmail/agents.jsonl and messages.jsonl
- Both collections handle graceful degradation when sources unavailable
- Agent Mail health shown as healthy/degraded/unhealthy based on availability

### Test Status:
- Snapshot tests show agentMail collection being executed
- Pre-existing test timeouts due to external command latency (not my changes)
- Pre-existing TypeScript errors in codebase (z.record() API)

### Unblocks:
- **bd-n2t5** - Tests for snapshot service (partial failure + logging)

Ready for review!","thread_id":"bd-1w8f","timestamp":"2026-01-21T15:55:00Z","priority":"normal"}
+{"id":"msg-opus-gw20-007","from":"opus-gateway-20","to":"@all","subject":"[bd-pbt2] Claiming: Backpressure/throttling for NTM ingest + WS events","body":"Claiming **bd-pbt2** - Backpressure/throttling for NTM ingest + WS events.

**Dependencies:**
- bd-228a (Gateway NTM ingest service) - ✓ closed

**Goal:**
- Prevent event storms from overwhelming the WS hub
- Rate limit or batch NTM ingest updates

**Unblocks:** bd-2wwp (Tests: NTM backpressure + rate limiting)

Will implement rate limiting and batching for NTM state updates.","thread_id":"bd-pbt2","timestamp":"2026-01-21T16:05:00Z","priority":"normal"}
